title: Berlin Rooftop Bars - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin's best rooftop bars are casual, not glossy.** The two most-loved ones, Klunkerkranich and Deck 5, sit on top of shopping-centre car parks. Pick the casual roof or the dressed-up sky bar depending on the evening you actually want.

**Klunkerkranich in Neukölln is the sunset classic.** A plant-filled roof garden on the Neukölln Arcaden car park on Karl-Marx-Straße, cheap-ish, with music and a wide western view. Seasonal with moving hours and a small evening entry fee, so check before you go.

**Monkey Bar in City West is the polished skyline choice.** Tenth floor of the 25hours Hotel by the Zoo (S+U Zoologischer Garten), free entry, pricier cocktails, big western view. Come early or reserve on sunny evenings.

**House of Weekend on Alexanderplatz is the central party roof.** A terrace staring at the TV Tower, right by the transport hub. A club by night; in summer the upper sun-deck opens earlier, often announced on social media on the day.

**Deck 5 in Prenzlauer Berg is a beach bar on a roof.** Real sand, deckchairs and cheap drinks on the Schönhauser Allee Arcaden (U+S Schönhauser Allee). Strongly seasonal and weather-dependent. Solar near Anhalter Bahnhof is the grown-up glass-lift option with a dress code.

**Chase the light, not the clock.** Berlin sunsets swing from about 21:30 in June to soon after 18:00 in late October. Arrive roughly 45 minutes before sunset, and remember most roofs are open-air and simply close when it rains.
